title: Add rAF flush helper and intermediate-state tests for Claude integration tests
analysis:
All four items described in the task are already implemented on main and all 5 tests pass:

1. `flushRaf()` helper exists at module scope (lines 16-19) — drains rafCallbacks via splice+forEach.
2. Test 'text-delta events update responseText via rAF flush without done event' (lines 160-203) fires two text-delta events, asserts responseText is empty before flush, calls flushRaf(), then asserts 'Hello world' is present and message is NOT complete.
3. `afterAll` block (lines 51-55) restores origRAF and origCancelRAF globals.
4. The done-event test (line 138) calls flushRaf() before asserting mid-state, explicitly exercising the rAF path before the done event fires.

No code changes are required. The executor should verify tests pass and mark the task done.

steps.description: Verify flushRaf() helper exists and drains rafCallbacks array — already present at lines 16-19 of claudeIntegration.test.ts

Verify test 'text-delta events update responseText via rAF flush without done event' exists — tests rAF batching path independently: sends deltas, asserts buffered state, flushes, asserts applied state, confirms message not complete
Verify afterAll block restores globalThis.requestAnimationFrame and globalThis.cancelAnimationFrame from origRAF/origCancelRAF — already present at lines 51-55
Verify the done-event test ('claude events flow through subscribeToClaudeEvents into store state') calls flushRaf() at line 138 before asserting mid-state responseText, documenting the rAF dependency
Run full test suite (npx vitest run src/__tests__/claudeIntegration.test.ts) to confirm all 5 tests pass — no implementation changes needed

design_decisions.decision: No code changes required — task already fully implemented on main
design_decisions.rationale: git diff main shows no changes to claudeIntegration.test.ts. All four task items (flushRaf helper, rAF-only test, afterAll cleanup, explicit flushRaf in done-event test) are present and all 5 tests pass. The task was likely completed by a prior agent and merged.

design_decisions.decision: flushRaf uses splice(0) to atomically drain the callback array
design_decisions.rationale: splice(0) removes and returns all elements in one operation, preventing double-execution if a callback itself schedules more work via requestAnimationFrame.

design_decisions.decision: afterAll conditionally restores globals only if originals existed
design_decisions.rationale: In JSDOM/happy-dom test environments, requestAnimationFrame may not exist natively (origRAF would be undefined). The conditional guard prevents setting globals to undefined.

reuse.where: gui/src/__tests__/claudeStore.test.ts uses identical pattern in beforeEach
reuse.how: Both test files define rafCallbacks array, rAF polyfill, and flush helper. The integration test defines them at module scope; the unit test in beforeEach.
mockListen captures handlers by event name into a Record, allowing tests to simulate Tauri events by calling capturedHandlers[eventName]({payload})
Store is created with callback injection for bridge integration, same pattern used in both unit and integration tests
